english: taṅ sub taṅ (tang sub tang) 2. — 4. to be suitable, practicable, to do, bstán-pa γcíg-la stón-pa γnyis mi _óṅ-pas (bstan pa gcig la ston pa gnyis mi 'ong pas) Glr. as two preceptors for one doctrine will not do; yúl-du lóg-pa mi _óṅ-bas (yul du log pa mi 'ong bas) Glr. as a journey home is not practicable; _o-yóṅ-ṅog ('o yong ngog) v. _o ('o); lás-la oṅ-bai bár-du (las la ong ba'i bar du) as long as he was fit for work; to go on well, to do well C., *da yóṅ-ṅa* (*da yong nga*) W. will it do now? — 5. when connected with verbs, it serves to indicate futurity, like the English auxiliaries shall and will, as becomes evident from such expressions as the following: _čī-ba nam yoṅ ča med ('chi ba nam yong cha med) Mil. when dying comes, i.e. when we shall die, is uncertain; mdog gyúr-ba _oṅ (mdog gyur ba 'ong) Glr. a change of colour is coming, i.e. the colour will, or is going to, change; _gró-ba mi yoṅ-bar _dug ('gro ba mi yong bar 'dug) Mil. I am not likely (_dug-pa ('dug pa), 4) to go there any more; *ťel-čè mi yoṅ* (*thel che mi yong*) W. he will not be put to shame, not be disappointed; also with the supine: srog daṅ brál-bar gyúr-du _oṅ (srog dang bral bar gyur du 'ong) Dzl. it will even come to his dying, it will be his death; zós-su _oṅ (zos su 'ong) Dzl. he will even get so far as to eat ...; ši-bar _oṅ (shi bar 'ong) he will die; still more free and popular are those turns, in which the gerund or the mere root is used: γnaṅ-ste _oṅ (gnang ste 'ong) Pth. he will assent to it, allow it; γcíg min kyaṅ γcíg yin-te _oṅ (gcig min kyang gcig yin te 'ong) Glr. if it is not the one, it will be the other; sleb yoṅ (sleb yong) he will come Mil. and in C. very common; yid-čés mi _oṅ (yid ches mi 'ong) they will not believe it; it is also used to express the passive voice, and the English to become, to grow, to get: šés-na ṅa γsod _óṅ-bas (shes na nga gsod 'ong bas) Glr. as I should be killed, if she heard of it; *zer yoṅ* (*zer yong*) C. so it is said, expressed, i.e. this is the usual way of expressing it; *pél-te yoṅ* (*phel te yong*) W. it is getting larger, increases; or with a noun: smin _oṅ (smin 'ong) Glr. it is growing ripe; rgyál-po _báṅs-su _oṅ (rgyal po 'bangs su 'ong) Ma. the king becomes a subject.

light, shine, brightness, šar (shar) flames up, shines, _pro ('pro) spreads, proceeds from; _od spró-ba ('od spro ba) to emit light, bkyé-ba (bkye ba) to spread Sch.; _od lham-mér mdzád-pa ('od lham mer mdzad pa) resp., to shine with a bright light Sch.; _od kéṅs-pa ('od kengs pa) filled with light Sch.; lús-la _od yód-pa (lus la 'od yod pa) self-luminous, a property of primeval man Glr.; nyi-_ód (nyi 'od) sun-light, zla-_ód (zla 'od) moon-light, skar-_ód (skar 'od) star-light Cs.; rnam-_od (rnam 'od) brightness of the night-heavens, zodiacal-light Cs.; me-_od (me 'od) fire-shine Cs.; lustre, brightness, of polished metal, _od byín-pa ('od byin pa) to elicit a gloss or lustre, to give a bright polish Sch.; metaphor. fair complexion, external beauty, *káṅ-pe 'od pélte yoṅ* (*kang pe 'od phel te yong*) the splendour of the house increases, *bud ča dug* (*bud cha dug*) declines, decays W.; _od daṅ ldán-pa ('od dang ldan pa) B., _ód-čan ('od can) 1. luminous, emitting light; 2. bright, polished. 3. light, *da 'od-čan ča yin* (*da 'od can cha yin*) W. now it will grow light. 4. of a fine colour, of a blooming appearance Glr. 5. beautiful, splendid, stately; _od-med ('od med), vulgo _od-med-kán ('od med kan), the contrary. Comp. _od-kór ('od kor) or skor (skor) a luminous circle Lex. — _od-dkar ('od dkar) 1. white light. 2. symb. num.: 1. — _ód-čan ('od can), v. above. — _od-dpag-méd ('od dpag med), अमिताभ, also snaṅ-ba-mťa-yás (snang ba mtha' yas) the fourth Dhyani-Buddha, v. saṅs-rgyás (sangs rgyas). — _ód-spro ('od spro) (or _pro? ('pro?)) light? — *ód-ťo* (*'od tho*) W., *ód-ťo tog* (*'od tho tog*) hold up the light! *ód-ťo bu* (*'od tho bu*) glow-worm, fire-fly; _od-_pro ('od 'pro) sometimes occurring in the names of gods. — _od-γzér ('od gzer) ray of light Dzl. and elsewh. frq.; _od-γzér-čan ('od gzer can) n. of a god, _od-γzér-čan-ma ('od gzer can ma) of a goddess Do. — _od-sruṅ ('od srung) n. p. 1. the human Buddha of the preceding period of the world. 2. a king of Tibet, son of Langdarma. — _od-γsál ('od gsal) 1. a bright light or gloss, _od-γsál mdaṅs daṅ ldán-pa ('od gsal mdangs dang ldan pa) very glossy, of leaves. 2. com. of the supernatural enlightening of the saints, _od-γsál-gyi ṅáṅ-nas γzigs-te ('od gsal gyi ngang nas gzigs te) Mil. knowing, beholding, by means of prophetic light.
cane, bamboo, _ód-ma tsal ('od ma tshal), वेणुवन, cane-grove; such a grove near Rajagriha was a favourite retreat of Buddha.
W. but (sed, autem); (not so often used as in English).

but, yet, notwithstanding Dzl. and elsewh., frq. in B.; rarely _on-yaṅ ('on yang) for it Mil.; it stands at the beginning of sentences, but is also preceded by a gerund with -kyi (-kyi), in which case it is almost pleonastic; Lexx. give उताहो as the
